inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        if inst.arg == 0:\n            self.append_prefix_inst(inst)\n            self.accept_prefix_inst = False\n        else:\n            assert not self.accept_prefix_inst\n\n    if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n\n        def BINARY_OP(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n            assert inst.arg is not None\n            return _binary_op_lookup[inst.arg](self, inst)\n\n    def PRECALL(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        pass\n\n    def KW_NAMES(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        kw_names = self.code_options[\"co_consts\"][inst.arg]\n        assert isinstance(kw_names, tuple)\n        for name in kw_names:\n            assert isinstance(name, str)\n        assert self.kw_names is None\n        self.kw_names = ConstantVariable.create(value=kw_names)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n    def PUSH_NULL(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self.push(NullVariable())\n\n    def _call(self, inst: Instruction, call_kw: bool = False) -> None:\n        # see https://docs.python.org/3.11/library/dis.html#opcode-CALL\n        # for convention\n        if call_kw:\n            # TOS is kw_names for CALL_KW instruction\n            assert sys.version_info >= (3, 13)\n            kw_names = self.pop()\n            assert isinstance(kw_names, TupleVariable) and kw_names.is_python_constant()\n            kw_names = kw_names.as_python_constant()\n        else:\n            kw_names = self.kw_names.value if self.kw_names else ()\n\n        assert inst.arg is not None\n        contents = self.popn(inst.arg + 2)\n        if sys.version_info >= (3, 13):\n            # NULL and callable swapped\n            fn = contents[0]\n            args = [] if isinstance(contents[1], NullVariable) else [contents[1]]\n        else:\n            if isinstance(contents[0], NullVariable):\n                fn = contents[1]\n                args = []\n            else:\n                fn = contents[0]\n                args = [contents[1]]\n\n        if kw_names:\n            args = args + contents[2 : -len(kw_names)]\n            kwargs_list = contents[-len(kw_names) :]\n            kwargs = dict(zip(kw_names, kwargs_list))\n            assert len(kwargs) == len(kw_names)\n        else:\n            args = args + contents[2:]\n            kwargs = {}\n\n        try:\n            # if call_function fails, need to set kw_names to None, otherwise\n            # a subsequent call may have self.kw_names set to an old value\n            self.call_function(fn, args, kwargs)\n        finally:\n            self.kw_names = None\n\n    @break_graph_if_unsupported(push=1)\n    def CALL(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self._call(inst)\n\n    def COPY(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        assert inst.arg is not None\n        self.push(self.stack[-inst.arg])\n\n    def SWAP(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        assert inst.arg is not None\n        self.stack[-1], self.stack[-inst.arg] = self.stack[-inst.arg], self.stack[-1]\n\n    JUMP_BACKWARD = jump\n    JUMP_BACKWARD_NO_INTERRUPT = jump\n\n    POP_JUMP_FORWARD_IF_TRUE = generic_jump(operator.truth, False)\n    POP_JUMP_BACKWARD_IF_TRUE = generic_jump(operator.truth, False)\n    POP_JUMP_FORWARD_IF_FALSE = generic_jump(operator.not_, False)\n    POP_JUMP_BACKWARD_IF_FALSE = generic_jump(operator.not_, False)\n\n    def CACHE(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        pass\n\n    def BEFORE_WITH(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self.setup_or_before_with(inst)\n\n    def setup_or_before_with(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        ctx = self.pop()\n        if not isinstance(\n            ctx, (ContextWrappingVariable, GenericContextWrappingVariable)\n        ):\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Unsupported context manager\",\n                context=f\"Attempted SETUP_WITH/BEFORE_WITH on {ctx}\",\n                explanation=f\"Dynamo does not know how to enter a `{ctx.python_type_name()}` context manager.\",\n                hints=[\n                    \"Avoid using the unsupported context manager.\",\n                    \"If the context manager seems like it should be supported (e.g. torch.set_grad_enabled), then \"\n                    \"it may be the case that it was created outside the compiled region, which Dynamo does not support. \"\n                    \"Supported context managers can cross graph break boundaries only if they are local non-closure \"\n                    \"variables, or are intermediate values.\",\n                    \"File an issue to PyTorch. Simple context managers can potentially be supported, \"\n                    \"but note that context managers can't be supported in general\",\n                ],\n            )\n\n        if (\n            isinstance(ctx, GenericContextWrappingVariable)\n            and not ctx.supports_graph_breaks()\n        ):\n            self.active_generic_context_managers.append(ctx)\n\n        # Need this redundant check for mypy\n        assert isinstance(\n            ctx, (ContextWrappingVariable, GenericContextWrappingVariable)\n        )\n\n        exit = WithExitFunctionVariable(\n            ctx,\n            inst.target,\n        )\n\n        if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n            # See create_call_resume_at for block stack details.\n            # Only push a block if the current instruction's block is a\n            # with block that is not nested in a try block - that is, the current\n            # instruction's block target is the same as the top block's target.\n            if inst.exn_tab_entry and (\n                not self.block_stack\n                or inst.exn_tab_entry.target is not self.block_stack[-1].target\n            ):\n                target = None\n            else:\n                assert self.next_instruction.exn_tab_entry is not None\n                target = self.next_instruction.exn_tab_entry.target\n        else:\n            target = inst.target\n\n        self.push(exit)\n\n        if target:\n            if isinstance(self, InstructionTranslator) or config.nested_graph_breaks:\n                self.block_stack.append(\n                    BlockStackEntry(inst, target, len(self.stack), ctx)\n                )\n            else:\n                self.block_stack.append(BlockStackEntry(inst, target, len(self.stack)))\n\n        self.push(ctx.enter(self))\n\n    def append_prefix_inst(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        assert self.accept_prefix_inst\n        self.prefix_insts.append(inst)\n\n    def MAKE_CELL(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        if sys.version_info >= (3, 12) and not self.accept_prefix_inst:\n            # In 3.12+, MAKE_CELL is not longer necessarily a prefix instruction.\n            # It can be generated by inlined comprehensions.\n            assert isinstance(self.symbolic_locals[inst.argval], NullVariable)\n            self.symbolic_locals[inst.argval] = (\n                self.output.side_effects.track_cell_new()\n            )\n        else:\n            self.append_prefix_inst(inst)\n\n    def COPY_FREE_VARS(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self.append_prefix_inst(inst)\n\n    def RETURN_GENERATOR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self.append_prefix_inst(inst)\n\n    # 3.12 opcodes\n    # BINARY/STORE_SLICE opcodes are broken down into\n    # BUILD_SLICE 2 and BINARY/STORE_SUBSCR\n\n    def END_FOR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        if sys.version_info >= (3, 13):\n            self.pop()\n        else:\n            self.popn(2)\n\n    def LOAD_FAST_CHECK(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        if istype(self.symbolic_locals.get(inst.argval, None), NullVariable):\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"LOAD_FAST_CHECK on uninitialized variable\",\n                context=inst.argval,\n                explanation=f\"Attempted to load uninitialized local variable {inst.argval}\",\n                hints=[*graph_break_hints.USER_ERROR],\n            )\n        self.LOAD_FAST(inst)\n\n    def LOAD_FAST_AND_CLEAR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        if inst.argval not in self.symbolic_locals:\n            self.push(NullVariable())\n        else:\n            self.LOAD_FAST(inst)\n        self.symbolic_locals[inst.argval] = NullVariable()\n\n    def LOAD_SUPER_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self.CALL_FUNCTION(dataclasses.replace(inst, argval=2))\n        assert inst.arg is not None\n        if inst.arg & 1:\n            self.LOAD_METHOD(inst)\n        else:\n            self._load_attr(inst)\n\n    def CALL_INTRINSIC_1(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        if inst.argval == 3:\n            # INTRINSIC_STOPITERATION_ERROR\n            self.STOPITERATION_ERROR(inst)\n        elif inst.argval == 5:\n            # INTRINSIC_UNARY_POSITIVE\n            self.UNARY_POSITIVE(inst)\n        elif inst.argval == 6:\n            # INTRINSIC_LIST_TO_TUPLE\n            self.push(TupleVariable(self.pop().force_unpack_var_sequence(self)))\n        else:\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Missing CALL_INTRINSIC_1 handler\",\n                context=f\"CALL_INTRINSIC_1 operand: {inst.argval}\",\n                explanation=f\"No handler implemented for CALL_INTRINSIC_1 {inst.argval} instruction.\",\n                hints=[*graph_break_hints.SUPPORTABLE],\n            )\n\n    def END_SEND(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        tos = self.pop()\n        self.pop()\n        self.push(tos)\n\n    # 3.13 opcodes\n    # fused instructions LOAD_FAST_LOAD_FAST, STORE_FAST_STORE_FAST, STORE_FAST_LOAD_FAST\n    # are broken down.\n    @break_graph_if_unsupported(push=1)\n    def CALL_KW(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self._call(inst, call_kw=True)\n\n    def TO_BOOL(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        # TO_BOOL only precedes a conditional jump or UNARY_NOT (see compile.c in CPython)\n        # So we can skip this instruction as long as we remember to codegen a TO_BOOL\n        # before conditional jumps/UNARY_NOT.\n        assert self.next_instruction.opname in (\n            \"POP_JUMP_IF_TRUE\",\n            \"POP_JUMP_IF_FALSE\",\n            \"UNARY_NOT\",\n        )\n\n    def SET_FUNCTION_ATTRIBUTE(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        flags = inst.arg\n        assert flags is not None\n        fn = self.pop()\n        assert isinstance(fn, NestedUserFunctionVariable)\n        attr = self.pop()\n\n        if flags & 0x08:\n            fn.closure = attr\n        elif flags & 0x04:\n            fn.annotations = attr\n        elif flags & 0x02:\n            fn.kwdefaults = attr\n        elif flags & 0x01:\n            fn.defaults = attr\n\n        self.push(fn)\n\n    def CONVERT_VALUE(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self.push(self._convert_value(self.pop(), inst.argval))\n\n    def FORMAT_SIMPLE(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self._format_value(ConstantVariable.create(\"\"), 0)\n\n    def FORMAT_WITH_SPEC(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self._format_value(self.pop(), 0)\n\n    def is_non_empty_graph(self) -> bool:\n        if self.output.count_calls() > 1:\n            # perf optimization only\n            self.is_non_empty_graph = lambda: True  # type: ignore[method-assign]\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    def format_frame_summary(\n        self, additional_stack_frames: Optional[list[Any]] = None\n    ) -> str:\n        if additional_stack_frames is None:\n            additional_stack_frames = []\n        return \"\".join(\n            traceback.format_list(\n                [self.frame_summary()] + list(reversed(additional_stack_frames))\n            )\n        )\n\n    def frame_summary(self) -> traceback.FrameSummary:\n        return traceback.FrameSummary(\n            getattr(self.f_code, \"co_filename\", \"<unknown>\"),\n            self.lineno,\n            getattr(self.f_code, \"co_name\", \"<unknown>\"),\n            lookup_line=False,\n        )\n\n    def is_co_filename_from_nn_modules(self) -> bool:\n        filename = getattr(self.f_code, \"co_filename\", \"<unknown>\")\n        nn_modules_pattern = re.compile(r\".*torch/nn/modules.*\")\n        return nn_modules_pattern.match(filename) is not None\n\n    def store_global_weakref_by_id(self, prefix: str, value: Any) -> str:\n        global_name = self.output.install_global_by_id(prefix, weakref.ref(value))\n        install_guard(\n            GlobalWeakRefSource(global_name).make_guard(GuardBuilder.WEAKREF_ALIVE)\n        )\n        return global_name\n\n    @property\n    def fake_mode(self) -> Optional[FakeTensorMode]:\n        return self.output.tracing_context.fake_mode\n\n    @contextlib.contextmanager\n    def strict_translation_mode(\n        self, check_fn: Callable[[VariableTracker], bool]\n    ) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"\n        Strict mode is enabled on a per-VariableTracker level depending on the return value of check_fn(node).\n        \"\"\"\n        prior = self.strict_checks_fn\n        self.strict_checks_fn = check_fn\n        try:\n            yield\n        finally:\n            self.strict_checks_fn = prior\n\n    def speculate(self) -> SpeculationEntry:\n        assert self.instruction_pointer is not None\n        assert self.instruction_pointer > 0\n        return self.speculation_log.next(\n            self.f_code.co_filename,\n            self.lineno,\n            self.instruction_pointer - 1,\n            self.instructions[self.instruction_pointer - 1],\n        )\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        output: OutputGraph,\n        instructions: list[Instruction],\n        f_locals: dict[str, Any],\n        f_globals: dict[str, Any],\n        f_builtins: dict[str, Any],\n        code_options: dict[str, Any],\n        symbolic_locals: dict[str, VariableTracker],\n        symbolic_globals: dict[str, VariableTracker],\n        symbolic_torch_function_state: SymbolicTorchFunctionState,\n        f_code: types.CodeType,\n        export: bool,\n        inline_depth: int,\n        speculation_log: SpeculationLog,\n        exn_vt_stack: ExceptionStack,\n        distributed_state: Optional[DistributedState],\n        # This determines whether to use the execution recorder.\n        closure: Optional[tuple[types.CellType]] = None,\n        package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.speculation_log = speculation_log\n        self.distributed_state = distributed_state\n\n        # Mutable state checkpointed by copy_graphstate()\n        self.output = output\n        self.symbolic_locals = symbolic_locals\n        self.symbolic_globals = symbolic_globals\n        self.symbolic_torch_function_state = symbolic_torch_function_state\n        # used to keep cell/freevars alive after pruning symbolic_locals (prune_dead_locals)\n        # in order to generate any nested closures\n        self.post_prune_cell_and_freevars = None\n        self.stack: list[VariableTracker] = []\n        self.instruction_pointer = 0\n        self.start_point = None\n        self.current_instruction = create_instruction(\"NOP\")\n        self.block_stack = []\n        # states before SETUP_WITH for checkpointing and fallback\n        self.active_generic_context_managers: list[GenericContextWrappingVariable] = []\n        self.lineno = -1\n        self.kw_names = None\n        self.accept_prefix_inst = True\n        self.prefix_insts = []\n        self.exn_vt_stack = exn_vt_stack\n\n        # Properties of the input/output code\n        self.instructions: list[Instruction] = instructions\n        self.indexof: dict[Instruction, int] = get_indexof(self.instructions)\n        self.f_locals: dict[str, Any] = (\n            f_locals  # needed for recording accessed locals for replay\n        )\n        self.f_globals: dict[str, Any] = f_globals\n        self.f_builtins: dict[str, Any] = f_builtins\n        self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = code_options\n        self.f_code: types.CodeType = f_code\n\n        # Execution record for replaying errors\n        if closure is not None and config.replay_record_enabled:\n            self.exec_recorder = ExecutionRecorder(\n                code=f_code, closure=closure, code_options=code_options\n            )\n        else:\n            self.exec_recorder = None\n        # Stack of module being parsed, current nn.module is at the end of ordered dict.\n        # The first field of tuple is the fully qualified name of current module\n        # in original hierarchy.  The second field is the type of current nn.module\n        self.nn_module_stack: dict[str, tuple[str, type[Any]]] = {}\n        self.num_calls: dict[str, int] = {}\n        # Flag to indicate whether tracing is used for export.\n        self.export = export\n        # NOTE: one_graph is used for export/fullgraph=True to always force errors on graph breaks.\n        # To toggle erroring/resuming on graph breaks during fullgraph=False compile, self.error_on_graph_break\n        # is used instead. Every step(), its value is updated to the global tls.error_on_graph_break.\n        # We mirror this value since cleanup may (correctly) inadvertently change tls.error_on_graph_break.\n        # This assumes that we cannot both trace a change to tls.error_on_graph_break and graph break on\n        # the same instruction.\n        self.one_graph = False\n        self.error_on_graph_break = False\n        # Also do not graph break when tracing resume function prologues\n        self.is_tracing_resume_prologue = False\n\n        self.current_speculation = None\n\n        self.strict_checks_fn = None\n\n        self.is_leaf_tracer = True\n        self.parent = None\n        self.debug_locals = []\n\n        self.package = package\n\n        if sys.version_info >= (3, 10):\n            from .resume_execution import (\n                CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR,\n                CO_COROUTINE,\n                CO_GENERATOR,\n                CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE,\n            )\n\n            if f_code.co_flags & (\n                CO_GENERATOR | CO_COROUTINE | CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE | CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR\n            ):\n                self.push(BuiltinVariable(None))\n\n        self.inline_depth = inline_depth\n        self.inconsistent_side_effects = False\n        self._constants_cache: list[Optional[ConstantVariable]] = [None] * len(\n            f_code.co_consts\n        )\n\n        self.is_trace_bytecode_log_enabled: Optional[bool] = (\n            trace_bytecode_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG)\n        )\n        self.is_trace_source_log_enabled: Optional[bool] = (\n            trace_source_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG)\n        )\n        linecache.lazycache(f_code.co_filename, f_globals)\n\n\nclass InstructionTranslator(InstructionTranslatorBase):\n    @staticmethod\n    def current_tx() -> InstructionTranslator:\n        return tls.current_tx\n\n    @contextlib.contextmanager\n    def set_current_tx(self) -> Any:\n        prior = getattr(tls, \"current_tx\", None)\n        tls.current_tx = self\n        try:\n            yield\n        finally:\n            tls.current_tx = prior\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        instructions: list[Instruction],\n        f_code: types.CodeType,\n        f_locals: dict[str, Any],\n        f_globals: dict[str, Any],\n        f_builtins: dict[str, Any],\n        closure: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]],\n        torch_function_mode_stack: Any,\n        code_options: dict[str, Any],\n        compiler_fn: Any,\n        one_graph: bool,\n        export: bool,\n        export_constraints: Any,\n        frame_state: Any,\n        speculation_log: SpeculationLog,\n        exn_vt_stack: ExceptionStack,\n        distributed_state: Optional[DistributedState],\n        package: Optional[CompilePackage],\n    ) -> None:\n        _step_logger()(\n            logging.INFO,\n            f\"torchdynamo start tracing {f_code.co_name} {code_options['co_filename']}:{code_options['co_firstlineno']}\",\n        )\n        super().__init__(\n            output=OutputGraph(\n                code_options,\n                compiler_fn,\n                self,\n                export,\n                export_constraints,\n                frame_state,\n                local_scope=f_locals,\n                global_scope=f_globals,\n                f_code=f_code,\n                torch_function_mode_stack=torch_function_mode_stack,\n                package=package,\n            ),\n            instructions=instructions,\n            f_locals=f_locals,\n            f_globals=f_globals,\n            f_builtins=f_builtins,\n            closure=closure,\n            code_options=code_options,\n            symbolic_locals={},  # set below\n            # A global var is inserted only after a STORE_GLOBAL happens to it\n            symbolic_globals={},\n            symbolic_torch_function_state=None,  # type: ignore[arg-type] # set below\n            f_code=f_code,\n            export=export,\n            inline_depth=0,\n            speculation_log=speculation_log,\n            exn_vt_stack=exn_vt_stack,\n            distributed_state=distributed_state,\n            package=package,\n        )\n\n        self._throw_if_in_functorch()\n\n        # as soon as we create the tracing context we should keep it active, so any calls\n        # into dynamo apis can rely on finding it\n        with tracing(self.output.tracing_context), self.set_current_tx():\n            self.one_graph: bool = one_graph\n            self.export = export\n            if self.export:\n                assert self.one_graph, (\n                    \"Export without one graph - something has gone wrong.\"\n                )\n\n            self.symbolic_locals = {}\n            # Populate `symbolic_locals` with non-cell variables.\n            cell_and_freevars: set[str] = set(self.cell_and_freevars())\n\n            dynamism = code_context.get_context(f_code).get(\"dynamism\", None)\n            for name, value in f_locals.items():\n                if name not in cell_and_freevars:\n                    local_dynamism = None\n                    if dynamism:\n                        local_dynamism = frozenset(dynamism.get(name, {}).items())\n                    var = LazyVariableTracker.create(\n                        value,\n                        LocalSource(\n                            name,\n                            is_input=True,\n                            dynamism=local_dynamism,\n                        ),\n                    )\n                    self.symbolic_locals[name] = var\n\n            # Populate `symbolic_locals` with cells created by this frame,\n            # effectively implementing the `MAKE_CELL` instructions.\n            side_effects = self.output.side_effects\n            for name in self.cellvars():\n                if name in f_locals:\n                    # This models cells that are also function inputs.\n                    value = f_locals[name]\n                    # NOTE: root frame inputs that are captured by a nested\n                    # function become special cell objects -- they exist in\n                    # `f_locals` as contents of the cells, rather than the cells\n                    # objects themselves.\n                    #\n                    # In Dynamo, we choose to represent such input cell objects\n                    # as newly created (rather than pre-existing) cell objects,\n                    # because\n                    #\n                    # 1. The reason for representing a pre-existing cell object\n                    # is to emit guard or codegen mutations. However, local\n                    # cells should never be used for guards. Moreover, at this\n                    # point these input cell objects should've never been\n                    # accessed by anyone else, since Dynamo intercepts the frame\n                    # right after its evaluation starts, i.e., right after these\n                    # cell objects are created. So they should have no external\n                    # reference, meaning no mutation needs to be propagated.\n                    #\n                    # 2. This conveniently allows codegen to prune away\n                    # mutations to these cells, unless they escape the frame.\n                    contents_source = LocalSource(\n                        name, is_input=True, is_derefed_cell_contents=True\n                    )\n                    contents_var: VariableTracker = LazyVariableTracker.create(\n                        value, contents_source\n                    )\n                    cell_var = side_effects.track_cell_new()\n                    side_effects.store_cell(cell_var, contents_var)\n                else:\n                    cell_var = side_effects.track_cell_new()\n                cell_var.local_name = name  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                self.symbolic_locals[name] = cell_var\n\n            # Populate `symbolic_locals` with cells captured by this frame,\n            # effectively implementing the `COPY_FREE_VARS` instruction.\n            assert closure is not None\n            for name, cell in zip(self.freevars(), closure):\n                cell_source = LocalCellSource(name)\n                contents_source = LocalSource(name, is_derefed_cell_contents=True)\n                try:\n                    contents_var = LazyVariableTracker.create(\n                        cell.cell_contents, contents_source\n                    )\n                except ValueError:\n                    # Cell has not yet been assigned\n                    contents_var = variables.DeletedVariable()\n                cell_var = side_effects.track_cell_existing(\n                    cell_source, cell, contents_var\n                )\n                cell_var.local_name = name  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                self.symbolic_locals[name] = cell_var\n\n            self.symbolic_torch_function_state = SymbolicTorchFunctionState(\n                torch_function_mode_stack\n            )\n\n            if export:\n                # export gets confused if we never realize unused inputs\n                # in export mode just eagerly realize everything\n                self.symbolic_locals = variables.LazyVariableTracker.realize_all(\n                    self.symbolic_locals\n                )\n\n    def _throw_if_in_functorch(self) -> None:\n        # Fallback to eager in case of a graph break inside vmap\n        eager = torch._dynamo.lookup_backend(\"eager\")\n        compiler_fn = inspect.getattr_static(\n            self.output.compiler_fn, \"compiler_fn\", self.output.compiler_fn\n        )\n        ci = torch._C._functorch.peek_interpreter_stack()\n        forbidden_keys = (\n            torch._C._functorch.TransformType.Vmap,\n            torch._C._functorch.TransformType.Grad,\n            torch._C._functorch.TransformType.Jvp,\n        )\n\n        if ci is not None and ci.key() in forbidden_keys and compiler_fn is not eager:\n            name = ci.key().name.lower()\n            msg = (\n                \"If you are reaching here, it means dynamo failed for one of the following reasons:\\n\"\n                # Calling a torch.compiled function\n                f\"- Calling torch.func.{name}(compiled_fn) function from eager mode is not supported. \"\n                f\"Ensure that torch.func.{name} is also wrapped within a torch.compile function. \"\n                \"For more information, see PyTorch issue #128711.\\n\"\n                # if it reaches here, it means Dynamo failed to inline a functorch function\n                f\"- torch.func.{name}(fn) requires the function to be inlined by dynamo\"\n            )\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Unsupported functorch tracing attempt\",\n                context=\"\",\n                explanation=msg,\n                hints=[],\n            )\n\n    def get_example_value(self, source: Source) -> Any:\n        if isinstance(source, LocalSource):\n            return self.f_locals[source.local_name]\n        if isinstance(source, GlobalSource):\n            return self.f_globals[source.global_name]\n        raise KeyError\n\n    def symbolic_locals_contain_module_class(self) -> bool:\n        for v in self.symbolic_locals.values():\n            if isinstance(v, UserDefinedClassVariable) and issubclass(\n                v.as_python_constant(), torch.nn.Module\n            ):\n                return True\n        return False\n\n    def replace_tos_if_return_is_generator(self) -> None:\n        if (\n            len(self.stack)\n            and (tos := self.stack[-1])\n            and isinstance(tos, LocalGeneratorObjectVariable)\n        ):\n            self.stack[-1] = ListIteratorVariable(\n                tos.force_unpack_var_sequence(self),\n                mutation_type=ValueMutationNew(),\n            )\n\n    def _return(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self.replace_tos_if_return_is_generator()\n        assert self.instruction_pointer is not None\n        assert self.start_point is not None\n        get_metrics_context().increment(\n            \"ir_count\", self.instruction_pointer - self.start_point\n        )\n\n        if (\n            not config.allow_empty_graphs\n            and self.output.count_calls() == 0\n            and not self.inconsistent_side_effects\n            and not self.symbolic_locals_contain_module_class()\n            and not self.export\n            and not self.one_graph\n            and not self.error_on_graph_break\n            and not self.is_tracing_resume_prologue\n        ):\n            raise exc.SkipFrame(\"because no content in function call\")\n\n        self.instruction_pointer = None\n        _step_logger()(\n            logging.INFO,\n            f\"torchdynamo done tracing {self.f_code.co_name} ({inst.opname})\",\n        )\n        log.debug(\"%s triggered compile\", inst.opname)\n        all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\n            self,\n            reason=GraphCompileReason(\n                \"return_value\", [self.frame_summary()], graph_break=False\n            ),\n            # the value to be returned\n            stack_pops=1 if inst.opname == \"RETURN_VALUE\" else 0,\n        )\n        # check that our stack/locals meta are correct:\n        # we should only be tracing 1 frame, and there should not be any NULLs on the stack\n        assert len(all_stack_locals_metadata) == 1\n        assert not all_stack_locals_metadata[0].stack_null_idxes\n        return_inst = (\n            create_instruction(\"RETURN_VALUE\")\n            if inst.opname == \"RETURN_VALUE\"\n            else create_instruction(\"RETURN_CONST\", argval=inst.argval)\n        )\n        # NOTE: does the stack need to be empty after the return?\n        self.output.add_output_instructions([return_inst])\n gb_type=\"Inline attempt with __self__\",\n                context=str(func),\n                explanation=\"Attempted to inline a function with the `__self__` attribute. \"\n                \"Dynamo is expected to decompose method calls into function calls with a `self` argument.\",\n                hints=[],\n            )\n\n        if isinstance(func, UserFunctionVariable) and \"_torchdynamo_disable_msg\", None\n            )\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Skip inlining `torch.compiler.disable()`d function\",\n                context=str(func.get_function()),\n                explanation=f\"Skip inlining function {func.get_function()} since it was wrapped \"\n                f\"with `torch.compiler.disable` (reason: is_inlined_call=True)\n        if result.skipped:\n            from torch._dynamo.variables.misc import produce_trampoline_autograd_apply\n\n            # _origin marks this as coming from an internal              )\n            fn_qualname = func.fn.__qualname__ if hasattr(func, \"fn\") else \"\"\n            hints = [\n                f\"Avoid calling the function `{fn_qualname}`.\",\n to PyTorch.\",\n                ]\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Attempted to inline function marked as skipped\",\n                context=f\"qualname: {fn_qualname}, name: function `{fn_qualname}` \"\n                \"should not be traced.\",\n                hints=hints,\n            )\n\n        return result\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def build_inline_tracer(\n        parent: Any,\n        func: VariableTracker,\n        args: list[VariableTracker],\n        kwargs: Any,\n    ) -> InliningInstructionTranslator:\n        assert isinstance(\n            func,\n            (\n                UserFunctionVariable,\n                NestedUserFunctionVariable,\n                LocalGeneratorFunctionVariable,\n                LocalGeneratorObjectVariable,\n            ),\n        )\n        code: types.CodeType = func.get_code()\n        result = None\n        tracing_ctx = parent.output.tracing_context\n\n        # Check if we have already identified this function to be inline-able.\n        # The exception is dont_skip_tracing flag which affects the inline\n        # behavior. If the flag is True, don't rely on previous results.\n        if not config.dont_skip_tracing and tracing_ctx:\n            if previous_result := tracing_ctx.previously_inlined_functions.get(\n                code, None\n            ):\n                result = previous_result\n\n        if result is None:\n            if isinstance(func, SkipFunctionVariable):\n                unimplemented_v2(\n                    gb_type=\"Attempted to inline function marked as skipped (SkipFunctionVariable)\",\n                    context=f\"Attempted to inline a SkipFunctionVariable {func}\",\n                    explanation=(\n                        \"Attempted to inline a function that was previously determined to be marked as intentionally skipped.\"\n                    ),\n                    hints=[],\n                )\n            result = InliningInstructionTranslator.check_inlineable(func)\n            assert result.skipped is False\n\n            if not config.dont_skip_tracing and tracing_ctx:\n                tracing_ctx.previously_inlined_functions[code] = result\n\n        try:\n            sub_locals = func.bind_args(parent, args, kwargs)\n        except TypeError as e:\n            # Wrap the general TypeError during bind_args() to the internal ArgsMismatchError with detailed info\n            raise ArgsMismatchError(  # noqa: B904\n                \"{reason}.\\n  func = {func}, args = {args}, kwargs = {kwargs}\".format(\n                    reason=str(e),\n                    func=f\"'{func.get_name()}' {func.get_filename()}:{func.get_code().co_firstlineno}\",\n                    args=[arg.python_type() for arg in args],\n                    kwargs=kwargs,\n                ),\n            )\n\n        for v in itertools.chain(sub_locals.values()):\n            if not isinstance(v, VariableTracker):\n                unimplemented_v2(\n                    gb_type=\"Encountered unconverted argument when attempting to inline\",\n                    context=f\"func: {func}, arg: {v}\",\n                    explanation=\"An argument to an inlined function was not successfully converted to a VariableTracker.\",\n                    hints=[*graph_break_hints.DYNAMO_BUG],\n                )\n\n        if code.co_name in (\"__setitem__\", \"__setattr__\") and not (\n            args and isinstance(args[0], variables.UserDefinedObjectVariable)\n        ):\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Unsupported __setitem__/__setattr__ inline attempt\",\n                context=f\"code name: {code.co_name}, args: {args}\",\n                explanation=f\"Attempted to inline {code.co_name} where first argument (self) is not a user-defined object.\",\n                hints=[],\n            )\n\n        suffix = \"\"\n        # bubble up the exception to the parent frame.\n            raise\n        except exc.SkipFrame as e:\n            msg = f\"SKIPPED INLINING {code}: {e}\"\n            log.debug(msg)\n            raise Unsupported(msg) from e\n        except Exception:\n            log.debug(\"FAILED INLINING %s\", code)\n            raise\n        finally:\n            parent.error_on_graph_break = self.error_on_graph_break\n\n        if self.output.should_exit:\n            # Merge symbolic_globals back if parent and child are in the same namespace\n            parent.symbolic_globals.update(self.symbolic_globals)\n\n        parent.inconsistent_side_effects |= self.inconsistent_side_effects\n\n        log.debug(\"DONE INLINING %s\", code)\n        self.output.tracing_context.traced_code.append(code)\n\n        if config.enable_faithful_generator_behavior or (\n            isinstance(self, InliningGeneratorInstructionTranslator)\n            and self.is_generator_from_ctx_manager\n        ):\n            if (\n                is_generator(code)\n                and isinstance(self, InliningGeneratorInstructionTranslator)\n                and self.generator_exhausted\n            ):\n                assert isinstance(self, InliningGeneratorInstructionTranslator)\n                # TODO generalize the check for other non-importable cases.\n        # https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/8421b03b16a4852a527256cb7cdce2ab2d318548/Lib/collections/__init__.py#L441-L447\n        if \"__name__\" in self.f_globals and not self.f_globals[\"__name__\"].startswith(\n            \"namedtuple_\"\n        ):\n            module_name = self.f_globals[\"__name__\"]\n            module_source = self.import_source(module_name)\n            if \"torch_package\" in module_name:\n                fglobals_value = (\n                    torch.package.package_importer._package_imported_modules[\n                        module_name\n                    ]\n                )  # This codepath handles\n            # subgenerator and lines up with this line in Python 3.10\n            # https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.10/Python/ceval.c#L2599\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Unreachable sub-generator code\",\n                context=\"\",\n                explanation=\"Should only be encountered while implementing generator support.\",\n                hints=[],\n            )\n\n        try:\n            val = tos.next_variable(self)\n        except (StopIteration, exc.ObservedUserStopIteration) as ex:\n            if isinstance(ex, exc.ObservedUserStopIteration):\n                exc.handle_observed_exception(self)\n\n            # The iterator is exhausted. Stop the loop and return.\n            self.pop()\n            self.push(ConstantVariable.create(ex.value))\n        else:\n            # Repeat the YIELD_FROM instruction in the next eval loop\n            assert (\n                isinstance(self.instruction_pointer, int)\n                and self.instruction_pointer > 0\n            )\n            self.instruction_pointer -= 1\n\n            self.push(val)\n            # Add the value to yield into generated_items and replace the top of the stack with None\n            self.YIELD_VALUE(inst)\n\n    def SEND(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        assert len(self.stack) >= 2\n        val = self.pop()\n        tos = self.stack[-1]\n        if isinstance(tos, (IteratorVariable, LocalGeneratorObjectVariable)) or (\n            isinstance(tos, UserDefinedObjectVariable)\n            and isinstance(tos.value, collections.abc.Iterator)\n        ):\n            if isinstance(val, ConstantVariable) and val.value is None:\n                try:\n                    val = tos.next_variable(self)\n                except (StopIteration, exc.ObservedUserStopIteration) as ex:\n                    # To implement SEND, we have to look at the implementation\n                    # when the iterator returns StopIteration. This translates to this code\n                    # 3.11: https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.11/Python/ceval.c#L2613-L2619\n                    # 3.12: https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.12/Python/bytecodes.c#L863-L866\n                    # The implementation is different in 3.11 and 3.12. In 3.12, we rely\n                    # on END_SEND to clean up. In 3.11, SEND does the cleanup as well.\n                    if sys.version_info < (3, 12):\n                        self.pop()  # Python 3.12 uses new opcode END_SEND\n                    self.push(ConstantVariable.create(ex.value))\n                    self.jump(inst)\n                else:\n                    self.push(val)\n            else:\n                # invoke send\n                # Unreachable code - if you hit this, you are implementing generator support and have\n                # lifted the `unimplemented(\"generator\")` in frame conversion.    def test_cuda_event_created_outside_of_graph(self):\n        user_stream = torch.cuda.Stream()\n        event = torch.cuda.Event()\n        foo = torch.empty((2, 2), device=\"cuda\")\n\n        def func(foo):\n            event.wait()\n            return foo + 1, event\n\n        x = torch.randn((1024, 1024), device=\"cuda\")\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n\n        def run_iters(fn, compile=False):\n            if compile:\n                fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n            for _ in range(10):\n                with torch.cuda.stream(user_stream):\n                    torch.mm(x, x, out=foo)\n                    event.record()\n                out = fn(foo)\n                # let `fn` finish reading `foo` before writing to it in the next\n                # iteration or `run_iters` call.\n                torch.cuda.current_stream().synchronize()\n            return out\n\n        ref = run_iters(func, compile=False)\n        res = run_iters(func, compile=True)\n        self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 3)\n\n    @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), 2)\n\n            x = torch.add(x, 3)\n\n            event = cur_stream.record_event()\n            event.query()\n\n            new_stream.wait_event(event)\n            with torch.cuda.stream(new_stream):\n                x = torch.add(x, 4)\n\n            new_event = torch.cuda.Event()\n            new_event.record(new_stream)\n\n            new_event.wait(cur_stream)\n            x = torch.add(x, 5)\n\n            # use new event to sync\n            new_event.synchronize()\n\n            x = torch.relu(x)\n            x = torch.cos(x)\n            return x\n\n        x = torch.randn((2, 2), device=\"cuda\")\n        cur_stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()\n        new_stream = torch.cuda.Stream()\n        ref = fn(x, cur_stream, new_stream)\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts, fullgraph=True)\n        res = opt_fn(x, cur_stream, new_stream)\n        self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n test_autograd_profiler_enabled(self):\n        def fn(x):\n            if torch.autograd._profiler_enabled():\n                return x + 1\n            else:\n                return x - 1\n\n            torch.autograd._disable_profiler()\n        assert not torch.autograd._profiler_enabled()\n        ref = fn(x)\n        res = opt_fn(x)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \"requires cuda\")\n    def test_autocast(self):\n        if not torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported():\n            raise unittest.SkipTest(\"requires module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n        real_device = real.device\n        real_dtype = real.dtype\n\n        graph, _ = torch._dynamo.export(module)(torch.tensor([[0.0, 0], [0, 0]]))\n        exported = forward(self, x):\n                a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n                b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n                d_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), test_autocast_cpu_graph_break(self):\n        class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n            def forward(self, x):\n                a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n                b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                d_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n\n                with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n                    e_float16 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n                    torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                    f_float16 = torch.mm(d_float32, e_float16)\n                return f_float16\n\n        module = MyModule()\n        real = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n        real_device = real.device\n      # Regression for: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/93890\n        def fn(x):\n            with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n                x = torch.mm(x, x)\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                x = torch.relu(x)\n            return x\n\n        x = torch.rand([4, 4])\n        self.assertEqual(x.dtype, torch.float32)\n        res = fn(x)\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        opt_res = opt_fn(x)\n        self.assertTrue(torch.allclose(res, opt_res))\n        self.assertEqual(res.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n        self.assertEqual(opt_res.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n\n    def test_autocast_cpu_graph_break_inner_fn(self):\n        class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n            @staticmethod\n            def mm_breaks(x, y):\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                return torch.mm(x, y)\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n                b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n\n                with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n                    torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                    with torch.autocast(\n                        device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16, enabled=False\n                    ):\n                        torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                        g_float32 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n                        with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n                            # Check that nested with non-inlineable function with graph break\n                            torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                            f_float16_1 = self.mm_breaks(a_float32, b_float32)\n                    # We remember to exit the inner autocast correctly to outer\n                    # even after graph breaks\n                    f_float16 = self.mm_breaks(a_float32, b_float32)\n                    assert f_float16.dtype == f_float16_1.dtype\n                return f_float16, g_float32\n\n        module = MyModule()\n        real_16, real_32 = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n        real_device_16 = real_16.device\n        real_dtype_16 = real_16.dtype\n        real_device_32 = real_32.device\n        real_dtype_32 = real_32.dtype\n\n        graph = torch.compile(module, backend=\"eager\")\n        out_16, out_32 = graph(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.device, real_device_16)\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, real_dtype_16)\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.device, real_device_32)\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.dtype, real_dtype_32)\n\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.device.type, \"cpu\")\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.device.type, \"cpu\")\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.dtype, torch.float32)\n\n    def test_autocast_graph_break_method(self):\n        class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self, bias):\n                super().__init__()\n                self.bias = bias\n\n            def mm_not_break(self, x, y):\n                return torch.mm(x, y) + self.bias\n\n            def mm_breaks(self, x, y):\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                return torch.mm(x, y) + self.bias\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n                b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n\n                with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n                    with torch.autocast(\n                        device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16, enabled=False\n                    ):\n                        g_float32 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n                    f_float16 = self.mm_breaks(a_float32, b_float32)\n\n                    assert (\n                        f_float16[0][0] == self.mm_not_break(a_float32, b_float32)[0][0]\n                    )\n                return f_float16, g_float32\n\n        module = MyModule(bias=torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16))\n\n        with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n            # Autocast doesn't work on addition, so we need the bias to be `bfloat16`\n            res = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.float32) + torch.rand(\n                (8, 8), device=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16\n            )\n            self.assertEqual(res.dtype, torch.float32)\n\n        real_16, real_32 = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n        real_device_16 = real_16.device\n        real_dtype_16 = real_16.dtype\n        real_device_32 = real_32.device\n        real_dtype_32 = real_32.dtype\n\n        graph = torch.compile(module, backend=\"eager\")\n        out_16, out_32 = graph(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.device, real_device_16)\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, real_dtype_16)\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.device, real_device_32)\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.dtype, real_dtype_32)\n\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.device.type, \"cpu\")\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n def test_autocast_decorator(self):\n        def autocast_func(orig_func):\n            @torch.amp.autocast(device_type=\"cuda\", dtype=torch.float16)\n            def new_fwd(*args, **kwargs):\n                return orig_func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n            return new_fwd\n\n        def autocast_func_cuda(orig_func):\n            @torch.autocast(device_type=\"cuda\", dtype=torch.float16)\n            def new_fwd(*args, **kwargs):\n                return orig_func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n            return new_fwd\n\n        def autocast_func_cpu(orig_func):\n            @torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.float16)\n            def new_fwd(*args, **kwargs):\n                return orig_func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n            return new_fwd\n\n        def mm(a, b):\n            return torch.mm(a, b)\n\n        mm_float16 = autocast_func(mm)\n        mm_float16_cuda = autocast_func_cuda(mm)\n        mm_float16_cpu = autocast_func_cpu(mm)\n\n        def fn(a, b):\n            return mm_float16(a, b), mm_float16_cuda(a, b), mm_float16_cpu(a, b)\n\n        a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n        b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n\n        ref = fn(a_float32, b_float32)\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)(fn)\n        res = opt_fn(a_float32, b_float32)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n        self.assertTrue(res[0].dtype == torch.float16)\n        self.assertTrue(res[1].dtype == torch.float16)\n\n    @parametrize(\n        \"Ctx\",\n def test_graph_break_inlining_grad(self):\n        def gn(z):\n            with torch.no_grad():\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                return torch.sin(z)\n\n        def fn(x, test_context_wrapping_grad_mode_decorator(self):\n        ctx_wrappers = [(torch.enable_grad, True), (torch.no_grad, False)]\n        for call in [True, False]:\n            for i in range(2):\n                torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n                ctx_wrapper, _ = ctx_wrappers[i]\n                ctx_wrapper_inverse, mode_inverse = ctx_wrappers[(i + 1) % 2]\n\n                def fn(x):\n                    def inner_func(x):\n                        return x.sin()\n\n                    with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n                        if call:\n                            inner_func = ctx_wrapper()(inner_func)\n                        else:\n                            inner_func = ctx_wrapper(inner_func)\n\n                        # Calling no_grad or enabled_grad should not mutate global state\n                        assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == mode_inverse\n\n                    with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n                        return inner_func(x)\n\n                x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=True)\n                opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n                self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n                self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n\n    def test_context_wrapping_grad_mode_nested_function_decorator(self):\n        ctx_wrappers = [(torch.enable_grad, True), (torch.no_grad, False)]\n\n        for call in [True, False]:\n            for i in range(2):\n                torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n                ctx_wrapper, _ = ctx_wrappers[i]\n                ctx_wrapper_inverse, mode_inverse = ctx_wrappers[(i + 1) % 2]\n\n                def fn(x):\n                    with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n                        if call:\n\n                            @ctx_wrapper()\n                            def inner_func(x):\n                                return x.sin()\n\n                        else:\n\n                            @ctx_wrapper\n                            def inner_func(x):\n                                return x.sin()\n\n                        # Calling no_grad or enabled_grad should not mutate global state\n                        assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == mode_inverse\n\n                    with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n                        return inner_func(x)\n\n                x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=True)\n                opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n                self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n                self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n\n    def test_context_wrapping_set_grad_enabled_nested_function(self):\n        modes = [True, False]\n        for decorator in [True, False]:\n            for i in range(2):\n                torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n                mode = modes[i]\n                mode_inverse = modes[(i + 1) % 2]\n\n                def fn(x):\n                    with torch.set_grad_enabled(mode_inverse):\n                        if decorator:\n\n                            @torch.set_grad_enabled(mode)\n                            def inner_func(x):\n                                return x.sin()\n\n                        else:\n\n                            def inner_func(x):\n                                return x.sin()\n\n                            inner_func = torch.set_grad_enabled(mode)(inner_func)\n\n                        # Consuming set_grad_enabled by calling it on a function\n                        # should not mutate global state\n                        assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == mode_inverse\n\n                    with torch.set_grad_enabled(mode_inverse):\n                        return inner_func(x)\n\n            x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=True)\n            opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n            self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n            self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n\n    def test_inactive_context_graph_break_local(self):\n        def fn(x):\n            x = x + 1\n            ctx =   cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n        self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n        self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n        self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\n\n    def test_inactive_context_graph_break_local_nullctx(self):\n        import contextlib\n\n        # test with context     backends = [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n\n        @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n        def check_backend_state_is_modified():\n            self.assertEqual(\n                set(torch.nn.attention._cur_sdpa_kernel_backends()),\n                set(backends),\n            )\n\n        def f(x):\n            with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(backends=backends, test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager_as_decorator(self):\n        SDPA_BACKEND_PRIORITY = [\n            torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH,\n            torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.EFFICIENT_ATTENTION,\n            torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.FLASH_ATTENTION,\n        ]\n\n        @torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(\n            backends=SDPA_BACKEND_PRIORITY, set_priority=True\n        )\n        def scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, *args, **kwargs):\n            return torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention(\n                q, k, v, *args, **kwargs\n            )\n\n        def f(x):\n            return scaled_dot_product_attention(x, x, x)\n\n        opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        x = torch.rand(16, 16, 64, 256, dtype=torch.float16)\n        ref = f(x)\n        res = opt_f(x)\n\n        self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n\n    # Regression test to make sure the value of set_priority is used correctly.\n    def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager_set_priority(self):\n        backends = [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n        default_priority = torch._C._get_sdp_priority_order()\n\n        @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n        def check_backend_priority(changed: bool):\n            self.assertEqual(\n                changed,\n                torch._C._get_sdp_priority_order() != default_priority,\n            )\n\n        def f(x):\n            with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(backends=backends, set_priority=True):\n                x = x + 1\n                check_backend_priority(changed=True)\n                x = x + 1\n\n            with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(backends=backends, set_priority=False):\n                x = x + 1\n                check_backend_priority(changed=False)\n                x = x + 1\n\n            return x\n\n        opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\")\n        opt_f(torch.randn(2, 2))\n\n    def test_torch_profiler_use_after_with_block(self):\n        counters.clear()\n\n        def fn(x):\n            with torch.profiler.profile() as p:\n                pass\n            p.profiler.kineto_results.experimental_event_tree()\n            return x + 1\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        x = torch.ones(1)\n        ref = fn(x)\n        res = opt_fn(x)\n        self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n        self.assertEqual(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]), 1)\n\n\nclass ContextlibContextManagerTests(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCase):\n    def setUp(self):\n        self._prev = torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib\n        self._u_prev = torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest\n        torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib = True\n        torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest = True\n\n    def tearDown(self):\n        torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib = self._prev\n        torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest = self._u_prev\n\n    def test_ctx_basic0(self):\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def set_default_dtype(dtype):\n            old_dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()\n            try:\n                torch.set_default_dtype(dtype)\n                yield\n            finally:\n                torch.set_default_dtype(old_dtype)\n\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=True)\n        def fn():\n            with set_default_dtype(torch.float64):\n                x = torch.tensor([3.0, 3.0 + 5.0j])\n            return x\n\n        y = fn()\n        self.assertEqual(y.dtype, torch.complex128)\n        graph = eager.graphs[0]\n        actual = torch.set_default_dtype(torch.float32);  set_default_dtype_1 = None\n        return (x,)\n\"\"\",\n        )\n\n    def test_ctx_basic1(self):\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def compute_sin(x):\n            try:\n                yield x.sin()\n            finally:\n                pass\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(x):\n            with compute_sin(x) as y:\n                return y.cos()\n\n        x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n        y = fn(x)\n        self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n\n    def test_change_parent_nonlocal_0(self):\n        # test if a nonlocal actually gets propagated\n        z = 0\n        k = 0\n\n        def create_ctx():\n            @contextmanager\n            def ctx(x):\n                nonlocal z\n                nonlocal k\n                try:\n                    k = 100\n                    yield x.sin()\n                finally:\n                    pass\n\n            return ctx\n\n        def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n            nonlocal z\n            with ctx(x) as y:\n                z = k\n                return y.cos()\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(x):\n            ctx = create_ctx()\n            return run_ctx(ctx, x)\n\n        x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n        y = fn(x)\n        self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n        self.assertEqual(z, 100)\n        self.assertEqual(k, 100)\n\n    def test_change_parent_nonlocal_1(self):\n        # test if finally is executed and it is reading the correct variable\n        z = 1\n        k = 2\n\n        def create_ctx():\n            @contextmanager\n            def ctx(x):\n                nonlocal z\n                nonlocal k\n                try:\n                    yield x.sin()\n                finally:\n                    k = z\n\n            return ctx\n\n        def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n            nonlocal z\n test_globals_change_in_other_file(self):\n        @contextmanager\n        def update_global_ctx():\n            global _variable, _variable1\n            try:\n                _variable += 1\n                _variable1 += 1\n                yield\n            finally:\n                pass\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(x):\n            with update_global_ctx():\n                pass\n\n            with test_functions.update_global_ctx(x) as a:\n                # Ensure that the updated global values are read\n                test_functions.constant3(2, 3)\n                return x * a * (_variable + _variable1 + test_functions._variable)\n\n        res = fn(torch.ones(10))\n        self.assertEqual(_variable, 1)\n        self.assertEqual(_variable1, 1)\n        # Ensure that the reconstructed bytecode updates the global value in the\n        # other file.\n        self.assertEqual(test_functions._variable, 1)\n        self.assertEqual(res, 3 * torch.ones(10))\n\n    def test_change_parent_global_0(self):\n        # test if a global actually gets propagated\n        global z_glb, k_glb\n        z_glb, k_glb = 0, @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def whoo(x):\n            y = x.tan()\n            try:\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                yield y\n            finally:\n                pass\n\n        def f(x):\n            y = x.sin()\n            with whoo(x) as z:\n                y += z.neg()\n            y += x.cos()\n            return y\n\n        x = torch.randn(2)\n        expected = f(x)\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n        out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n        self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n        # no graph will be generated as we will skip all frames due to the graph break\n        self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n\n    def test_graph_break_inside_ctx_with_side_effects(self):\n        L = []\n\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def whoo(x):\n            y = x.tan()\n            try:\n                L.append(x.sin())\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                yield y\n            finally:\n                L.append(x.cos())\n\n        def f(x):\n            y = x.sin()\n            with whoo(x) as z:\n                y += z.neg()\n            y += x.cos()\n            return y\n\n        x = torch.randn(2)\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n        y = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n        self.assertEqual(y, x.sin() + x.tan().neg() + x.cos())\n        self.assertEqual(L, [x.sin(), x.cos()])\n        # no graph will be generated as we will skip all frames due to the graph break\n        self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n\n    def test_graph_break_inside_ctx_1(self):\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def whoo(x):\n            y = x.tan()\n            try:\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                yield y\n            finally:\n                pass\n\n        def bar(x):\n            with whoo(x) as z:\n                return z.neg()\n\n        def f(x):\n            return x.sin() + bar(x) + x.cos()\n\n        x = torch.randn(2)\n        expected = f(x)\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n        out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n        self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n        self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 2)\n        self.assertExpectedInline(\n            normalize_gm(eager.graphs[0].print_readable(False)),\n            \"\"\"\\\nclass GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n    def forward(self, L_x_: \"f32[2]\"):\n        l_x_ = L_x_\n\n        sin: \"f32[2]\" = l_x_.sin();  l_x_ = None\n        return (sin,)\n\"\"\",\n        )\n        self.assertExpectedInline(\n            normalize_gm(eager.graphs[1].print_readable(False)),\n            \"\"\"\\\nclass GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n    def forward(self, L_stack0_: \"f32[2]\", L_stack1_: \"f32[2]\", L_x_: \"f32[2]\"):\n        l_stack0_ = L_stack0_\n        l_stack1_ = L_stack1_\n        l_x_ = L_x_\n\n        add: \"f32[2]\" = l_stack0_ + l_stack1_;  l_stack0_ = l_stack1_ = None\n        cos: \"f32[2]\" = l_x_.cos();  l_x_ = None\n        add_1: \"f32[2]\" = add + cos;  add = cos = None\n        return (add_1,)\n\"\"\",\n        )\n\n    def test_graph_break_inside_ctx_2(self):\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def whoo(x):\n            try:\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                yield x.cos()\n            finally:\n                pass\n\n        def g(x):\n            return x.neg() + x.acos()\n\n        def f(x):\n            y = x.sin()\n            with whoo(x) as z:\n                y += g(z)\n            y += y.tan()\n            return y\n\n        x = torch.randn(2)\n        expected = f(x)\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n        out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n        self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n        self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 1)\n\n    def test_graph_break_before___enter__(self):\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def whoo(x):\n            try:\n                yield x + 1\n            finally:\n                pass\n\n        def fn(x):\n            ctx = whoo(x)\n            torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n            y = ctx.__enter__()\n            ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n            return y\n\n        x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n        with self.assertRaises(InternalTorchDynamoError):\n            torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)(x)\n\n    def test_graph_break_in_finally(self):\n        z = []\n\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def whoo(x):\n            nonlocal z\n            try:\n                z.append(x)\n                yield x.sin()\n            finally:\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                z.append(x.cos())\n\n        def fn(x):\n            ctx = whoo(x)\n            y = ctx.__enter__()\n            ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n            return y\n\n        x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n        out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(x)\n        self.assertEqual(out, x.sin())\n 1\n    # img_x, img_y have shapes (N, w), (N, h)\n\n    gx = img_x[:, None, :].expand(N, img_y.size(1), img_x.size(1))\n    gy = img_y[:, :, None].expand(N, img_y.size(1), img_x.size(1))\n    grid = torch.stack([gx, gy], dim=3)\n\n    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        if not masks.dtype.is_floating_point:\n            masks = masks.float()\n    img_masks = F.grid_sample(masks, grid.to(masks.dtype), align_corners=False)\n\n    if skip_empty and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        return img_masks[:, 0], (slice(y0_int, y1_int), slice(x0_int, x1_int))\n    else:\n        return img_masks[:, 0], ()\n\n\ndef global_fn(x):\n    return torch.sin(x)\n\n\ndef cat(tensors, dim=0):\n    # from detectron2 wrappers.py\n    assert isinstance(tensors, (list, tuple))\n    if len(tensors) == 1:\n        return tensors[0]\n    return torch.cat(tensors, dim)\n\n\ndef shapes_to_tensor(x, device=None):\n    # from detectron2 wrappers.py\n    if torch.jit.is_scripting():\n        return torch.as_tensor(x, device=device)\n    if torch.jit.is_tracing():\n        assert all(isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in x), (\n            \"Shape should be tensor during tracing!\"\n        )\n        # as_tensor should not be used in tracing because it records a constant\n        ret = torch.stack(x)\n        if ret.device != device:  # avoid recording a hard-coded device if not necessary\n            ret = ret.to(device=device)\n        return ret\n    return torch.as_tensor(x, device=device)\n\n\nfw_graph = [None]\nbw_graph = [None]\n\n\ndef aot_graph_capture_backend(gm, tensor: torch.Tensor):\n        \"\"\"\n        Args:\n            tensor (Tensor[float]): a Nx4 matrix.  Each row is (x1, y1, x2, y2).\n        \"\"\"\n        device = (\n            tensor.device if isinstance(tensor, torch.Tensor) else torch.device(\"cpu\")\n        )\n        tensor = torch.as_tensor(tensor, dtype=torch.float32, device=device)\n        if tensor.numel() == 0:\n            # taken from modeling_reformer.py in huggingface\n    @staticmethod\n    def forward(\n        ctx,\n        hidden_states,\n        layers,\n        attention_mask,\n        head_mask,\n        num_hashes,\n        all_hidden_states,\n        all_attentions,\n        past_buckets_states,\n        use_cache,\n        orig_sequence_length,\n        output_hidden_states,\n        output_attentions,\n    ):\n        all_buckets = ()\n\n        # split duplicated tensor\n        hidden_states, attn_output = torch.chunk(hidden_states, 2, dim=-1)\n\n        for layer in layers:\n            if output_hidden_states is True:\n                all_hidden_states.append(hidden_states)\n\n            attn_output = layer(attn_output)\n            all_buckets = all_buckets + (attn_output,)\n\n        # Add last layer\n        if output_hidden_states is True:\n            all_hidden_states.append(hidden_states)\n\n        # attach params to ctx for backward\n        ctx.save_for_backward(attn_output.detach(), hidden_states.detach())\n        ctx.layers = layers\n        ctx.all_buckets = all_buckets\n        ctx.head_mask = head_mask\n        ctx.attention_mask = attention_mask\n\n        # Concatenate 2 RevNet outputs\n        return torch.cat([attn_output, hidden_states], dim=-1)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def backward(ctx, grad_hidden_states):\n        grad_attn_output, grad_hidden_states = torch.chunk(\n            grad_hidden_states, 2, dim=-1\n        )\n\n        # free memory\n        del grad_attn_output\n\n        # num of return vars has to match num of forward() args\n        # return gradient for hidden_states arg and None for other args\n        return (\n            grad_hidden_states,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n        )\n\n\nclass ReformerEncoder(torch.nn.Module):\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.dropout = 0.5\n        self.layer_norm = torch.nn.LayerNorm(512, eps=1.0e-12)\n        self.layers = [torch.nn.Linear(256, 256)]\n\n    def forward(\n        self,\n        hidden_states,\n        attention_mask=None,\n        head_mask=[None] * 6,\n        num_hashes=None,\n        use_cache=False,\n        orig_sequence_length=64,\n        output_hidden_states=False,\n        output_attentions=False,\n    ):\n        # hidden_states and attention lists to be filled if wished\n        all_hidden_states = []\n        all_attentions = []\n        past_buckets_states = [((None), (None)) for i in range(len(self.layers))]\n\n        # concat same tensor for reversible ResNet\n        hidden_states = torch.cat([hidden_states, hidden_states], dim=-1)\n        hidden_states = _ReversibleFunction.apply(\n            hidden_states,\n            self.layers,\n            attention_mask,\n            head_mask,\n            num_hashes,\n            all_hidden_states,\n            all_attentions,\n            past_buckets_states,\n            use_cache,\n            orig_sequence_length,\n            output_hidden_states,\n            output_attentions,\n        )\n\n        # Apply layer norm to concatenated hidden states\n        hidden_states = self.layer_norm(hidden_states)\n\n        # Apply dropout\n        hidden_states = torch.nn.functional.dropout(\n            hidden_states, p=self.dropout, training=self.training\n        )\n\n        return ReformerEncoderOutput(\n            hidden_states=hidden_states,\n            all_hidden_states=all_hidden_states,\n            all_attentions=all_attentions,\n            past_buckets_states=past_buckets_states,\n        )\n\n\nclass ListConfig:\n    class ValueNode:\n        def __init__(self, value):\n            self.value = value\n\n        def _dereference_node(self):\n            return self\n\n        def _is_missing(self):\n            return False\n\n        def _value(self):\n            return self.value\n\n    # Based on an example from omegaconfig.listconfig\n    class ListIterator(Iterator[Any]):\n        def __init__(self, lst: Any, resolve: bool) -> None:\n            self.resolve = resolve\n            self.iterator = iter(lst.__dict__[\"_content\"])\n            self.index = 0\n\n        def __next__(self) -> Any:\n            x = next(self.iterator)\n            if self.resolve:\n                x = x._dereference_node()\n                if x._is_missing():\n                    raise AssertionError\n\n            self.index = self.index + 1\n            if isinstance(x, ListConfig.ValueNode):\n                return x._value()\n            raise AssertionError\n\n    def __iter__(self):\n        return self._iter_ex(True)\n\n    def _iter_ex(self, resolve: bool) -> Iterator[Any]:\n        try:\n            return ListConfig.ListIterator(self, resolve)\n        except Exception:\n            raise AssertionError from None\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._content = [\n            ListConfig.ValueNode(1),\n            ListConfig.ValueNode(3),\n            ListConfig.ValueNode(torch.tensor([7.0])),\n        ]\n\n\ndef longformer_chunk(hidden_states, window_overlap=256):\n    \"\"\"convert into overlapping chunks. Chunk size = 2w, overlap size = w\"\"\"\n\n    # non-overlapping chunks of size = 2w\n    hidden_states = hidden_states.view(\n        hidden_states.size(0),\n        hidden_states.size(1) // (window_overlap * 2),\n        window_overlap * 2,\n        hidden_states.size(2),\n    )\n\n    # use `as_strided` to make the chunks overlap with an overlap size = window_overlap\n    chunk_size = list(hidden_states.size())\n    chunk_size[1] = chunk_size[1] * 2 - 1\n\n    chunk_stride = list(hidden_states.stride())\n    chunk_stride[1] = chunk_stride[1] // 2\n    return hidden_states.as_strided(size=chunk_size, stride=chunk_stride)\n\n\nclass PartialT5(torch.nn.Module):\n    # Highly simplified T5Attention prefix\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.q = torch.nn.Linear(512, 512)\n        self.k = torch.nn.Linear(512, 512)\n        self.v = torch.nn.Linear(512, 512)\n\n    def forward(\n        self,\n        hidden_states,\n        key_value_states=None,\n        past_key_value=None,\n        query_length=None,\n    ):\n        batch_size, seq_length = hidden_states.shape[:2]\n\n        real_seq_length = seq_length\n\n        if past_key_value is not None:\n            assert len(past_key_value) == 2, (\n                f\"past_key_value should have 2 past states: keys and values. Got {len(past_key_value)} past states\"\n            )\n            real_seq_length += (\n                past_key_value[0].shape[2] if query_length is None else query_length\n            )\n\n        def shape(states):\n            \"\"\"projection\"\"\"\n            return states.view(batch_size, -1, 8, 64).transpose(1, 2)\n\n        def project(hidden_states, proj_layer, key_value_states, past_key_value):\n            \"\"\"projects hidden states correctly to key/query states\"\"\"\n            if key_value_states is None:\n                # self-attn\n                # (batch_size, n_heads, seq_length, dim_per_head)\n                hidden_states = shape(proj_layer(hidden_states))\n            elif past_key_value is None:\n                # cross-attn\n                # (batch_size, n_heads, seq_length, dim_per_head)\n                hidden_states = shape(proj_layer(key_value_states))\n\n            if past_key_value is not None:\n                if key_value_states is None:\n                    # self-attn\n                    # (batch_size, n_heads, key_length, dim_per_head)\n                    hidden_states = torch.cat([past_key_value, hidden_states], dim=2)\n                else:\n                    # cross-attn\n                    hidden_states = past_key_value\n            return hidden_states\n\n        # get query states\n        query_states = shape(\n            self.q(hidden_states)\n        )  # (batch_size, n_heads, seq_length, dim_per_head)\n\n        # get key/value states\n        key_states = project(\n            hidden_states,\n            self.k,\n            key_value_states,\n            past_key_value[0] if past_key_value is not None else None,\n        )\n        value_states = project(\n            hidden_states,\n            self.v,\n            key_value_states,\n            past_key_value[1] if past_key_value is not None else None,\n        )\n\n        # compute scores\n        scores = torch.matmul(query_states, key_states.transpose(3, 2))\n\n        # (truncated here )\n        return scores, value_states\n\n\nclass ChunkReformerFeedForward(torch.nn.Module):\n    # simplified from HF modeling_reformer.py\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.layer_norm = torch.nn.LayerNorm(256, eps=1e-12)\n        self.dense = torch.nn.Linear(256, 256)\n       )\n\n    def forward_chunk(self, hidden_states):\n        hidden_states = self.layer_norm(hidden_states)\n        hidden_states = self.dense(hidden_states)\n        return
